label: Tiny Listeners
metaTitle: Stories for 2-3 Year Olds — AI Story Generator | Playla
metaDescription: Free AI story generator for 2-3 year olds. Simple, colorful bedtime stories with repetition and gentle rhythms. Generate and listen instantly.
characteristics: Very simple vocabulary, short sentences, lots of repetition, bright imagery, familiar objects, soothing rhythms. Stories should be 1-2 minutes long.
parentingTips: Point to things in the story and name them — 'Look, a dog! What does a dog say?'
Use different voices for different characters to keep little ones engaged
Don't worry about finishing the story — follow your child's attention
Repetition is golden at this age. Reading the same story 10 times is perfectly normal and beneficial.
samplePrompts: A story about a sleepy bunny going to bed
A puppy who makes friends at the park
A star who falls asleep in a child's hand

label: Story Starters
metaTitle: Stories for 3-4 Year Olds — AI Story Generator | Playla
metaDescription: Free AI story generator for 3-4 year olds. Engaging tales with simple plots, lovable characters, and gentle lessons. Generate and listen instantly.
characteristics: Simple plots with clear beginnings and endings, familiar settings (home, school, park), lovable animal characters, gentle humour, basic emotions.
parentingTips: Ask 'What do you think happens next?' to build prediction skills
Let your child 'read' the pictures back to you
Connect story events to your child's life: 'Remember when we saw a butterfly too?'
This is the perfect age to start a bedtime story routine
samplePrompts: A little bear who tries to count all the stars
A duck who wants to learn to swim
A magic crayon that draws real things

label: Imagination Explorers
metaTitle: Stories for 4-5 Year Olds — AI Story Generator | Playla
metaDescription: Free AI story generator for 4-5 year olds. Imaginative tales with moral lessons, silly humor, and relatable characters. Generate and listen instantly.
characteristics: Imaginative premises, moral lessons woven naturally into plots, silly humour, relatable characters (often animals), basic problem-solving in the story.
parentingTips: Ask about the character's feelings: 'How do you think the fox felt?'
Encourage your child to retell the story in their own words
Discuss the moral naturally: 'What did the bear learn?'
Let kids choose the next story topic — it builds ownership and excitement
samplePrompts: A cat who becomes the teacher for a day
A dinosaur who visits a modern school
A child who finds a tiny door in the garden

label: Young Adventurers
metaTitle: Stories for 5-6 Year Olds — AI Story Generator | Playla
metaDescription: Free AI story generator for 5-6 year olds. Adventure stories with richer vocabulary, fun twists, and characters who solve problems. Generate and listen instantly.
characteristics: Richer vocabulary with context clues, multi-step plots, characters who solve problems, gentle suspense, introduction of broader themes like teamwork and empathy.
parentingTips: Introduce 'chapter' stories that span multiple nights to build anticipation
Ask 'What would you do if you were this character?'
Use new vocabulary from the story in daily conversation
Encourage your child to draw their favourite scene from the story
samplePrompts: A kid who finds a magic map in their backpack
A baby dragon's first day at dragon school
Pirates who discover their treasure is a library

label: Independent Readers
metaTitle: Stories for 6-8 Year Olds — AI Story Generator | Playla
metaDescription: Free AI story generator for 6-8 year olds. Chapter-length adventures with complex plots, mystery, and humor. Generate and listen instantly.
characteristics: Complex plots with subplots, diverse character motivations, introduction of mystery and suspense, humour with wordplay, stories that challenge without overwhelming.
parentingTips: Let them read independently but discuss the story afterwards
Introduce different genres to find what they love most
Talk about character motivations: 'Why did she make that choice?'
This is a great age for stories that model problem-solving and resilience
samplePrompts: A detective duo who solves mysteries at school
A robot who joins a human family
A fantasy world hidden inside a video game

label: Deep Divers
metaTitle: Stories for 8-10 Year Olds — AI Story Generator | Playla
metaDescription: Free AI story generator for 8-10 year olds. Sophisticated adventures with emotional depth, moral complexity, and exciting twists. Generate and listen instantly.
characteristics: Sophisticated narratives with emotional depth, moral ambiguity, multi-layered characters, real-world themes explored through fiction, longer attention span supported.
parentingTips: Discuss themes and moral dilemmas: 'Was the character right to do that?'
Encourage them to write their own stories inspired by what they read
Introduce them to series that build world-knowledge over multiple stories
This age loves stories where kids are the heroes solving adult-sized problems
samplePrompts: A kid who discovers their town has a secret underground history
A time-traveller stuck in a loop trying to fix one mistake
An AI that becomes self-aware inside a kid's computer

label: Story Masters
metaTitle: Stories for 10-12 Year Olds — AI Story Generator | Playla
metaDescription: Free AI story generator for 10-12 year olds. Complex narratives with nuanced themes, plot twists, and mature emotional depth. Generate and listen instantly.
characteristics: Complex narratives with nuanced themes, unreliable narrators, plot twists, exploration of identity and belonging, stories that respect the reader's intelligence, can handle darker tones appropriately.
parentingTips: Respect their reading preferences — this is the age where genre loyalty forms
Discuss books like peers, not like teacher-student
Introduce graphic novels and different narrative formats
Stories at this age often process real anxieties (peer pressure, identity) — let them
samplePrompts: A teen who discovers they're a character in someone else's story
A horror mystery set in a boarding school
A group of friends who find a portal in the school library
